no issues detected.\n\n**Report 2:**\n- **Summary:** Similar to Report 1, it describes a standard autogenerated factory, with no suspicious patterns.\n- **Code confirmation:** The code is typical for deploying and connecting to a smart contract, with no signs of malicious behavior.\n- **Scores:** Malware 0, obfuscated 0, risk 0.1 — consistent.\n- **Conclusion:** Valid and accurate.\n\n**Report 3:**\n- **Summary:** Again, a standard ethers.js factory class with no malicious patterns.\n- **Code confirmation:** The functions and static data are typical; static ABI and bytecode.\n- **Scores:** Malware 0, obfuscated 0, risk 0.1 — consistent.\n- **Conclusion:** Accurate.\n\n**Report 5:**\n- **Summary:** Same as above; boilerplate code for contract deployment, no malicious activity.\n- **Code confirmation:** No signs of obfuscation, secrets, or malicious logic.\n- **Scores:** Malware 0, obfuscated 0, risk 0 — consistent.\n- **Conclusion:** Correct.\n\n---\n\n### Overall assessment:\n- **Presence of issues in code:** The code is a standard, autogenerated ethers.js factory class with static ABI and bytecode. No malicious code, no dynamic data, no external data sources, and no obfuscation.\n- **Errors or flaws in reports:** All reports correctly identify the benign nature of the code.
